creator: kris@obsecurity.org
text:
On Fri, Mar 02, 2007 at 08:47:06AM -0500, Kris Kennaway wrote:
> On Fri, Mar 02, 2007 at 06:06:44AM +0000, Edwin Groothuis wrote:
> > Synopsis: NO_PACKAGE handling by ports build system
> > 
> > Responsible-Changed-From-To: freebsd-ports-bugs->portmgr
> > Responsible-Changed-By: edwin
> > Responsible-Changed-When: Fri Mar 2 06:06:28 UTC 2007
> > Responsible-Changed-Why: 
> > over to portmanager for analysis
> > 
> > http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=109745
> 
> I looked at this a while ago, and ISTR the problem is that this is
> currently used inconsistently.  Some ports abuse it to mean
> "RESTRICTED + NO_CDROM", so a careful license audit is needed of the
> ports that are marked NO_PACKAGE but do not have both of those also
> set, before it can be enabled to work as documented.

Actually I think while the above is still true, the root reason is
that NO_PACKAGE builds are not even attempted on pointyhat, so nothing
is run to collect distfiles.

There is additional ambiguity here: some ports use it to mean "this
port should not be packaged (but it would work if you tried)" and some
ports use it to mean "the port cannot be packaged".  I started trying
to fix this several years ago but became entangled in a web of trying
to untangle related ambiguities in the restriction variables to do
with licensing.

Kris
